decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Burning Inquiry and Faithless Looting to power out Hollow One and use it alongside other aggressive creatures like Detective's Phoenix and Marauding Mako to deal damage quickly. Additionally, using cards like Street Wraith and Nethergoyf to further fuel the graveyard and benefit from the self-discarding effects of the deck. The inclusion of cards like Unholy Heat and Lightning Bolt provide additional removal and reach to close out games. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and aggressive strategy.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive early creature plays, efficient removal, and card advantage to overwhelm opponents quickly. By leveraging powerful cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, alongside support from Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to apply pressure and close out games before the opponent can stabilize. With a solid mana base and versatile spells like Thraben Charm and Arena of Glory to support the aggressive strategy, this deck is designed to consistently push for victory in Modern 1 vs 1 matchups.

The deck aims to quickly generate mana through spells like Desperate Ritual and Pyretic Ritual, with the help of cards like Ruby Medallion and Ral, Monsoon Mage. It then uses this mana to cast a high number of spells in a single turn, leading to a massive storm count that can finish the game with cards like Grapeshot. The deck also utilizes card draw effects like Reckless Impulse and Glimpse the Impossible to help find combo pieces quickly, along with Past in Flames to recur spells from the graveyard for additional value.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Splinter Twin combo to create infinite creature tokens. It includes cards that support the combo by providing card draw, protection, and disruption for the opponent. The deck also includes some utility cards like Prismatic Ending and Consult the Star Charts to deal with threats and improve consistency. By assembling the combo pieces and protecting them effectively, the deck aims to win the game through overwhelming the opponent with an army of token creatures.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck includes efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and versatile cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to maintain board control. Cards like Goblin Bombardment and Arena of Glory provide additional ways to deal damage and buff your creatures. The deck also includes some utility creatures like Voice of Victory and Ranger-Captain of Eos to provide flexibility and options in different game situations. Overall, this deck aims to be aggressive and proactive in its gameplay style.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures and spells to deal damage to the opponent quickly and efficiently. By using cards like Monastery Swiftspear, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Slickshot Show-Off along with burn spells like Lava Dart and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to pressure the opponent and close out the game before they can stabilize. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation with cards like Expressive Iteration and Preordain to ensure consistency in drawing threats and answers.

This deck aims to control the game by assembling UrzaTron (Urza's Tower, Mine, and Power Plant) to generate massive amounts of mana. It utilizes powerful Eldrazi creatures like Devourer of Destiny, Thought-Knot Seer, and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger, along with planeswalkers like Ugin, the Eye of Storms and Karn, the Great Creator, to maintain board control and disrupt opponents' strategies. With cards like Kozilek's Command and Sowing Mycospawn, the deck can handle various threats and outvalue opponents in the long game while also ramping up mana for big plays.

This deck focuses on assembling a variety of creatures to create a toolbox of options that can be used in combination with powerful combo cards like Yawgmoth, Thran Physician and Malevolent Rumble to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes creatures with persist and undying abilities to easily recur and sacrifice for value, as well as cards like Green Sun's Zenith to tutor for specific creatures as needed. The goal is to generate incremental value and outmaneuver the opponent through efficient creature interactions and combo plays.

This deck focuses on generating a large amount of red mana quickly through rituals and medallions, to cast powerful spells like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Past in Flames multiple times. By utilizing cards like Reckless Impulse and Wish, it aims to draw through the deck rapidly to find key combo pieces. Valakut Awakening and Escape to the Wilds provide additional card advantage, while Grapeshot serves as a finisher. The deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a storm of spells in a single turn, using the Ruby Storm archetype to achieve victory.

This deck focuses on aggressive gameplay by utilizing a combination of powerful creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy involves playing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Territorial Kavu, supported by spells like Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial, to maintain control of the board and deal direct damage to the opponent. The inclusion of Leyline cards and Leyline Binding helps to further support this aggressive strategy by providing additional benefits and protection for your creatures. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and finish the game quickly.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly using the UrzaTron lands to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures and control spells. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon are key cards for board control and disruption, while Thought-Knot Seer provides hand disruption. Devourer of Destiny and Glaring Fleshraker offer threats on the battlefield. The deck also includes Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere to slow down opponent's plays. Overall, the deck aims to control the game through mana advantage and powerful Eldrazi threats.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with Utopia Sprawl and Talisman of Impulse to play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker. Control elements are utilized through cards like Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Eldrazi Temple and Sanctum of Ugin help to cast Eldrazi spells more easily. The deck also includes removal spells like Dismember and Unholy Heat to deal with threats on the board. Overall, the strategy revolves around overwhelming the opponent with big creatures and controlling the battlefield.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. The deck focuses on establishing control of the game by efficiently handling threats, disrupting the opponent's game plan, and generating card advantage through spells like Consult the Star Charts and Wan Shi Tong, Librarian. By controlling the board and resources, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through attrition and strategic decision-making.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using Devoted Druid and Vizier of Remedies to generate infinite mana. This combo is supported by cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Leyline of Abundance to tutor for key pieces and ramp up mana production. The deck also utilizes a creatures toolbox approach, including cards like Badgermole Cub and Walking Ballista for flexibility and control. By leveraging these key cards and maximizing mana efficiency, the deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with large creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth for a fast and decisive victory.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with rituals and Ruby Medallion, while using Ral, Monsoon Mage as the main win condition. The goal is to play out cards like Glimpse the Impossible or Reckless Impulse to draw through the deck and find the combo pieces needed to storm off and deal lethal damage with Grapeshot. Past in Flames allows for recursion and additional copies of key spells, while Wish provides additional utility. The deck aims to be fast and explosive, overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells and burn damage.

The deck focuses on using instant speed reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to cheat out powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard. By combining this with cards like Ephemerate and Faithful Mending to manipulate the graveyard and draw cards, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. Additionally, disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation help to protect the combo and control the game, while Prismatic Ending provides removal for opposing threats.
